Question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?

Answer: I am a 23 year old female who grew up in a small suburb in the west valley of Phoenix. Because of this, I never wanted to be a small town girl. As soon as I turned 18, I had the opportunity to move to California for 6 months and I have never looked back. I love new experiences and have been privileged enough to visit 32 countries. And as the US citizen, that has been the most valuable lessons that I have learned while abroad. Moving from country to country can be difficult at times but will never stop me from making a move when opportunity knocks.

After returning to the states, I traveled to New York, Las Vegas, Washington D.C, New Orleans, Los Angeles, and San Diego as a tour guide on on a 3 month road trip. I was able to complete my undergraduate work with a double major in Journalism and English with a focus in Professional Writing. Over the past few years, I have had the opportunity to hone in my skills and help numerous people. From teaching history to high school students to one on one mentoring to younger females, I have loved sharing my life experience with them.

As I travel and work towards being an English language teacher, I am hoping to prepare them for life in our ever changing and sophisticated global society. Furthermore, I have aided both graduate and undergraduate students with their essays. I have tutored children aged 7-17 in English Language Arts. And I also have a background in proofreading for numerous businesses in England.

Question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?

As soon as I turned 18, I had the opportunity to move to California for 6 months and I have never looked back. I love new experiences and have been privileged enough to visit 32 countries. And as the US citizen, that has been the most valuable lessons that I have learned while abroad. Moving from country to country can be difficult at times but will never stop me from making a move when opportunity knocks.

After returning to the states, I traveled to New York, Las Vegas, Washington D.C, New Orleans, Los Angeles, and San Diego as a tour guide on on a 3 month road trip. I was able to complete my undergraduate work with a double major in Journalism and English with a focus in Professional Writing. Over the past few years, I have had the opportunity to hone in my skills and help numerous people. From teaching history to high school students to one on one mentoring to younger females, I have loved sharing my life experience with them.

As I travel and work towards being an English language teacher, I am hoping to prepare them for life in our ever changing and sophisticated global society. Furthermore, I have aided both graduate and undergraduate students with their essays. I have tutored children aged 7-17 in English Language Arts. And I also have a background in proofreading for numerous businesses in England.

I just wanted to give a little background on myself, answer what you wanted to know. I’d say I’m most proud of my ability to stick with things I start. I kind of quit a lot of stuff that I start, whether it be learning how to run or a university, but I don’t think I’m a quitter. There’s just only so much time in life and sometimes you just have to decide between a million different things you want to do and stop worrying about it.

I worked for 35 years as an educator, starting as a teacher in LAUSD then moving to what was then called the University of Southern California School of Education. I was there until 1991, then moved to National University where I worked 13 years until retiring in 2004. I currently teach part time at CSUF and write a blog every week. Rosalie and I love to travel, go to movies, read the newspaper, enjoy our two grandchildren, watch CNN, eat organic food, and try to fix up a vintage house in Fullerton. The house has become a labor of love. We have a dog and 6 chickens in the back yard, fresh organic eggs every day. We have lived in the same house in Fullerton for 40 years. I have four sons and two daughter-in-laws, six grandchildren, and one great-granddaughter on the way! I have spent the past 10 years as a baseball dad, volunteering as a team parent for our boys' baseball teams. We’ve traveled all over the country for tournaments. My youngest boy has just signed a scholarship with Dixie State University in St. George, Utah.
